title: Berlin's Walls Begin to Crumble
story: Families torn apart by the Berlin Wall received hope in 1963. On December 17, East and West Berlin signed a groundbreaking agreement. For the first time since the wall's construction, West Berliners could visit relatives in the East. Though brief, these reunions brought joy to many separated loved ones. The accord marked a small step towards healing the divided city, hinting at the possibility of future unity.
activity_question: What did the agreement between East and West Berlin allow?
activity_choices: West Berliners could visit relatives in the East
The Berlin Wall was torn down
East Berliners could move to the West
A new subway system was built
activity_answer: West Berliners could visit relatives in the East
follow_up_question: How do you think families felt when they could finally see each other again?

title: Unearthing the Aztec Sun Stone
story: Buried beneath Mexico City's bustling streets lay a hidden treasure. On December 17, 1790, workers digging in the city's main square stumbled upon an enormous stone disk. title: Funky Beats Begin: Art Neville's Birthday
story: Groovy tunes were in the air when Art Neville was born in 1937. This funky musician from New Orleans would grow up to rock the keyboard and sing soulful R&B. Art became famous with his family band, the Neville Brothers, and later jammed with The Funky Meters. His musical journey began on December 17, setting the stage for decades of rhythm and blues magic.
activity_question: What type of music was Art Neville known for?
bonus_fact: Art Neville's hometown, New Orleans, is often called the birthplace of jazz music.
